prerequisites: A Google Workspace domain, since the Directory API lets administrators view and manage their organization's users, groups, devices, and related resources.
A Google Workspace administrator account with sufficient privileges to authorize access to directory data.
A Google Cloud project with the Admin SDK API enabled and an OAuth consent screen configured.
OAuth 2.0 client credentials (client ID and client secret) for the OAuth method, or a service account key JSON for the Service Account Key method.
For the service account path: a service account with domain-wide delegation of authority, authorized for the read-only Directory scopes.

authMethods.name: Sign in via Google (OAuth 2.0)
authMethods.summary: Authorize with OAuth 2.0 using credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and a credentials.refresh_token. The Directory API requires OAuth 2.0; configure the OAuth consent screen and grant the needed Directory scopes when generating the refresh token.

authMethods.name: Service Account Key
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a service account by supplying credentials.credentials_json. The service account must have domain-wide delegation of authority granted by a Google Workspace administrator and be authorized for the read-only Directory scopes (admin.directory.user.readonly and admin.directory.group.readonly).

setupSteps.title: Create a Google Cloud project and enable the Admin SDK API
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud console, create or select a project and enable the Admin SDK API. The Directory API requires OAuth 2.0 authorization, so configure the OAuth consent screen and declare the Directory authorization scopes your sync needs.

setupSteps.title: Choose OAuth or Service Account credentials
setupSteps.body: Decide which credential type to use. Use OAuth to authorize directly through Google's OAuth flow, or use a Service Account Key for a non-interactive setup. Both map to the connector's single credentials config object.

setupSteps.title: For OAuth: create client credentials and obtain a refresh token
setupSteps.body: Create an OAuth 2.0 client ID and secret in Google Cloud, then complete the Google authorization flow to obtain a refresh token. Provide credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, and credentials.refresh_token to the connector.

setupSteps.title: For Service Account: delegate domain-wide authority and export the key
setupSteps.body: Create a service account and grant it domain-wide delegation of authority, authorizing the read-only scopes https://www.googleapis.com/auth/admin.directory.user.readonly and https://www.googleapis.com/auth/admin.directory.group.readonly. A Google Workspace administrator must perform this delegation. Download the service account key JSON and supply it as credentials.credentials_json.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Populate the credentials configuration with the chosen secret fields: client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token for OAuth, or credentials_json for the service account. Verify the connection so the connector can read directory users and groups.

sources.title: Directory API authorization and OAuth 2.0 scopes
